title: Ensure that the RotateKubeletServerCertificate argument is set to true

input_row.title: Ensure that the RotateKubeletServerCertificate argument is set to true
input_row.description: Enable kubelet server certificate rotation on controller-manager.
input_row.rationale: RotateKubeletServerCertificate causes the kubelet to both request a serving certificate after bootstrapping its client credentials and rotate the certificate as its existing credentials expire. This automated periodic rotation ensures that the there are no downtimes due to expired certificates and thus addressing availability in the CIA security triad. Note: This recommendation only applies if you let kubelets get their certificates from the API server. In case your kubelet certificates come from an outside authority/tool (e.g. Vault) then you need to take care of rotation yourself. Impact: None
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-controller-manager Verify that RotateKubeletServerCertificate argument exists and is set to true.
input_row.remediation: Edit the Controller Manager pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- controller-manager.yaml on the Control Plane node and set the --feature-gates parameter to include RotateKubeletServerCertificate=true. --feature-gates=RotateKubeletServerCertificate=true Default Value: By default, RotateKubeletServerCertificate is set to "true" this recommendation verifies that it has not been disabled. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kubelet-tls-bootstrapping/#approval-controller 2. https://github.com/kubernetes/features/issues/267 3. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/45059 4. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-controller-manager/

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_controller_manager_rotate_kubelet_server_certificate_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the kube-controller-manager Pod specification, typically located in the kube-system namespace as a static Pod manifest (commonly at /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-controller-manager.yaml).
- Use kubectl to retrieve the running kube-controller-manager Pod and inspect its command-line arguments.
    - Command: kubectl -n kube-system get pod -l component=kube-controller-manager -o json
    - Parse the .spec.containers[].command or .spec.containers[].args fields for the --feature-gates flag.
- Validate that the --feature-gates argument includes RotateKubeletServerCertificate=true.
    - If --feature-gates is present, ensure RotateKubeletServerCertificate is set to true (either as the only value or among other feature gates).
    - If --feature-gates is absent, check documentation for the default (per CIS, default is true, but explicit presence is preferred for auditability).
- Pass Criteria: The kube-controller-manager is running with --feature-gates including RotateKubeletServerCertificate=true, or the default applies and is not overridden to false.
- Fail Criteria: The --feature-gates argument is present and sets RotateKubeletServerCertificate to false, or the argument is missing and the default is overridden elsewhere.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.11.1 PDF
step2_review:

- Parsing the `.spec.containers[].command` or `.spec.containers[].args` fields for the `--feature-gates` flag is accurate.
- The program name could be more descriptive. It should clearly indicate the specific security check being performed. The current name is somewhat generic.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Documentation Check:** While the assessment mentions checking documentation for defaults, it could be improved by specifying how to verify if defaults are overridden elsewhere, such as through configuration management tools or environment variables.
- **Alternative Methods:** Consider using Kubernetes ConfigMaps or other configuration management tools if applicable, to ensure that the settings are consistently applied across all environments.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-structured and uses appropriate Kubernetes commands and logic to verify the control. The approach is sound and aligns with best practices for Kubernetes security audits.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify

- Modify the program name to be more descriptive as suggested.
- Enhance the assessment by explicitly detailing how to verify if defaults are overridden, potentially through additional configuration files or environment variables.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**
Both AI opinions agree that the control can be audited using automated methods via the Kubernetes API and `kubectl` CLI. The control involves checking the command-line arguments of the kube-controller-manager, which can be efficiently done through automation. The rationale for automation is supported by the ability to programmatically verify the presence and value of the `--feature-gates` argument.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
k8s_controller_manager_feature_gates_rotate_kubelet_server_certificate_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Kubernetes Resource/Service: kube-controller-manager
- API/CLI Method: kubectl -n kube-system get pod -l component=kube-controller-manager -o json
- Properties to Check: .spec.containers[].command or .spec.containers[].args for the `--feature-gates` flag
- Pass Criteria: The `--feature-gates` argument includes RotateKubeletServerCertificate=true, or the default applies and is not overridden to false.
- Fail Criteria: The `--feature-gates` argument is present and sets RotateKubeletServerCertificate to false, or the argument is missing and the default is overridden elsewhere.
